Equity, derivatives, commodity, currency trading + institutional services and investment banking. Brokerage now contributes only 30% of total revenue, down from historical highs.
Mutual funds and PMS. 91% of AUM outperforming benchmarks over 3 years.
HNI/UHNI advisory. Family count up 41% YoY.
Retail mortgage lending — affordable and mid-segment.

28x P/E on FY27E EPS of INR 50; ARR > 70%, AMC +40% YoY.
24x P/E on FY27E EPS of INR 47; ARR stable at 65%, steady AUM.
18x P/E on depressed FY27E EPS of INR 35; sustained regulatory headwinds.
ARR > 70%, AMC AUM +40% YoY, regulatory clarity.
ARR steady at 65%, AUM compounding, stable regulation.
Sustained F&O headwinds, fintech share loss.

ARR at 61% of net revenues; fee-based at 45%. H1FY26 operating PAT INR 1,088 Cr (+11% YoY) on net revenue INR 2,888 Cr (+15%). Asset-light fee model scales without proportional cost growth. F&O constraints limit broking scalability near-term.
AMC AUM +33% YoY to INR 1.89L Cr with INR 11,610 Cr net flows.

16.57L HNWIs by 2027; India 4th-largest private wealth market by 2028 (+58.4% UHNWIs). Sector profits to nearly double by FY30. MF penetration at 5–6%. Massive TAM with adjacency expansion across capital markets, wealth, housing, alternatives.
India private credit hit USD 9.0 Bn in H1 2025 (+53% YoY).

91% of AUM outperforming benchmarks over 3 years gives pricing power in PMS/wealth. Brokerage revenue down 24% from regulatory changes — fee compression in cyclical segment. Discount brokers and fintech limit pricing in commoditized broking.
Diversified mix offset 24% brokerage decline, validating partial pricing-power hedge.

MCap INR 49,079 Cr (+24.8% YoY); P/E 24.2; P/B 3.82. Strong RoCE; disciplined allocation into high-return fee businesses. Reinvestment in tech, talent, distribution, research generates sustainable advantage.
PWM AUM +31% YoY to INR 1.96L Cr; family count +41%; PAT +14% (9M).

Q2FY25 PAT INR 1,242 Cr (+123% YoY), 46% revenue growth, 48% RoE — recovery and execution. Q2FY26 PAT -67.6% YoY, revenue -34.9% YoY shows residual cyclicality from F&O regulation. Continuity across cycles maintained.
Despite repeated SEBI interventions, AUM and ARR base continue to compound — institutional resilience.

Raamdeo Agrawal: QGLP creator, 'Buy Right, Sit Tight' framework. Navin Agarwal (since 2000): business strategy and capital allocation. Ajay Menon (27+ yrs): operations, compliance, risk, technology. Founding duo across nearly four decades — rare DNA in Indian financial services.
Rashtriya Samman Patra recipient (top tax-payer FY95–FY99) signals early ethical commercial success.

Consensus TP INR 1,126.67 (max INR 1,200) vs CMP INR 822.90 — 37% upside; market not yet pricing ARR re-rating. Credit rating upgraded to AA+ (Stable) by ICRA. ARR > 70% would trigger re-rating from broking to asset-management multiples within 6–18 months.
ARR at 65% of net revenues (up from 61%); management guiding higher — measurable inflection.
INR 7 lakh penalty for margin reporting, collection violations, and other regulatory lapses found during inspection.
INR 25 lakh fine for misutilization of client funds and inaccurate margin reporting.
Accused alongside other top brokers of misselling NSEL contracts, KYC manipulation, client code modification, and illegal transactions per SFIO/EOW findings.
Declared 'not fit and proper' as commodity derivative broker in Feb 2019 based on SFIO/EOW recommendations.
13 NSE and 9 BSE terminals operating from undeclared locations including an unreported branch in Delhi.
Co-founder (1987). Creator of the QGLP investment process and 'Buy Right, Sit Tight' framework. Built one of India's most respected investment research franchises. Widely cited investment thinker.

Co-founder (1987). Led the firm's transformation from broking house into a comprehensive financial services platform. Recipient of Rashtriya Samman Patra for being among the highest taxpayers FY95–FY99.
Joined 2000. Drives business strategy and capital allocation across the Group, building market-leading positions through disciplined deployment.
Joined 1998. 27+ years in Indian capital markets. Cornerstone of operational excellence and technology initiatives across the Group.
